title: RCA Annotation
description: Human annotation of a root-cause-analysis output, capturing correctness, evidence traceability, difficulty, and alternative diagnoses.

job_id.description: The AAP job ID being annotated.

annotated_at.description: ISO 8601 timestamp of when the annotation was created.

category_correct.description: Whether the agent's root cause category was correct.

category_comment.description: Explanation of why the category is correct or what it should be.

summary.description: One sentence describing what failed and why.

summary_accurate.description: Whether the agent's summary accurately described the root cause.

summary_comment.description: Explanation of what is accurate or what should be corrected.

data_sources_used.description: Tracks which analysis step outputs were available and whether they contributed evidence to the annotation. Surfaces gaps so reviewers know which data sources were missing.

used_in_evidence.description: Whether any evidence item references step1.

note.description: Why this source was unavailable or unused, if applicable.

available.description: Whether step2_splunk_logs.json contained usable data (not empty/errored).

used_in_evidence.description: Whether any evidence item references step2.

note.description: Why this source was unavailable or unused, e.g. 'Splunk not configured', 'GUID query failed', 'No OCP logs found'.

available.description: Whether step3_correlation.json contained usable correlation data (not AAP-only with no Splunk data).

used_in_evidence.description: Whether any evidence item references step3.

note.description: Why this source was unavailable or unused, e.g. 'AAP-only, no Splunk data to correlate', 'Low confidence correlation'.

used_in_evidence.description: Whether any evidence item references step4.

note.description: Why this source was unavailable or unused, if applicable.

evidence.description: Evidence items with full traceability. Exactly one item must have is_root_cause: true.

source.description: Which analysis step the evidence comes from.

source_file.description: Relative path to the analysis file, e.g. .analysis/<job_id>/step1_job_context.json

json_path.description: Dot/bracket path to the value within the source file, e.g. failed_tasks[0].duration

exact_value.description: Literal value at the json_path (use for numbers, booleans, short strings).

exact_quote.description: Verbatim text at the json_path (use for log lines, error messages, config snippets).

line_number.description: Optional line number within the source file.

github_path.description: GitHub file reference in format 'owner/repo:path/to/file.yml' or 'owner/repo:path/to/file.yml:line'.

message.description: Human-readable description of what this evidence shows.

is_root_cause.description: True for the single evidence item that most directly caused the failure.

evidence_feedback.description: Free-text feedback on the completeness or accuracy of the evidence set.

difficulty.description: Overall difficulty of diagnosing this failure.

difficulty_score.description: Numeric difficulty score (0-10) derived from the rubric.

difficulty_justification.description: Step-by-step rubric breakdown showing how the score was reached.

difficulty_appropriate.description: Whether the agent's stated difficulty matches the rubric score.

action.description: Specific, actionable step to fix or prevent the issue.

file.description: File to change, or 'N/A' for manual operations.

contributing_factors.description: Conditions that made the failure more likely or harder to diagnose.

alternative_diagnoses.description: Plausible alternative root causes that were considered and ruled out.

summary.description: One sentence describing the alternative diagnosis.

why_wrong.description: Which evidence contradicts this diagnosis or why it was ruled out.

plausibility.description: How convincing this alternative was before being ruled out.

supporting_evidence.description: Evidence items that superficially support this diagnosis.

contradicting_evidence.description: Evidence items that rule this diagnosis out.
